f.steps:
1. Fresh install
2. Launch the app
f.expected: Welcome/Get Started screen shows with both Sign up and Log in options.

f.steps:
1. Tap Sign up
2. Enter email, password, first name, last name; pick nationality from dropdown
3. Submit
f.expected: Account created; a 6-digit verification code is emailed; verification screen shows.

f.steps:
1. Complete sign up
2. Enter the emailed 6-digit code
f.expected: Verification succeeds; user proceeds to rank selection.

f.steps:
1. From Welcome, tap Log in
2. Enter valid email + password
f.expected: User lands in the app (dashboard/wallet).

f.steps: 1. Log in with a valid email but wrong password
f.expected: Clear, professional error message; no crash; no access.
f.notes2: Regression watch: after the failed attempt, tap outside the fields — the keyboard must dismiss (P0 bug filed 14 Jul).

f.steps:
1. From Log in, tap Forgot password
2. Enter account email
3. Follow the reset flow end to end
4. Sign in with the new password
f.expected: Reset completes; old password stops working; new password signs in.

f.steps:
1. From Welcome, choose Google login
2. Complete the Google flow with a test account
f.expected: Account created/signed in via Google; onboarding continues normally.

f.steps:
1. From Welcome, choose Apple login
2. Complete Sign in with Apple
f.expected: Account created/signed in via Apple; onboarding continues normally.

f.steps: 1. After verification, reach 'Choose your current rank'
f.expected: Main categories shown: Officer, Rating, Galley/Hotel Department, Staff Crew, Technical Support & Roles.

f.steps:
1. After rank, reach 'Choose Your Vessel Type'
2. Open Commercial Cargo Ships
3. Open Tanker Ships
f.expected: Commercial Cargo lists its 12 subtypes (Container … Log Carrier); Tanker lists Oil Tanker (+ any others built).

f.steps: 1. Complete rank + vessel selection
f.expected: User reaches the app with a basic profile reflecting rank + vessel choices.

f.steps:
1. Fresh account (no certs)
2. Open the Certificates tab
f.expected: 'No Certificates Yet' secure-wallet message; Add Certificate CTA; Search Certificates field; bottom nav = Certificates / Sea Service Records / Profile.

f.steps:
1. Open Certificates
2. Use the upload form to add a certificate file
3. Assign it to a category/folder
f.expected: Certificate uploads, appears in the chosen folder, opens in the viewer.

f.steps:
1. Open Certificates
2. Choose scan
3. Scan a physical document
4. Save to a folder
f.expected: Scan captures legibly and saves as a document in the chosen folder.

f.steps: 1. On populated My Certificates, review the category folders
f.expected: Five categories: Travel Documents, Licensing Certifications, Regulatory Statutory Documents, Industry Required, Other — colour-coded, each opening its own list.

f.steps: 1. Add 3+ certificates to the same category
f.expected: All entries listed and individually openable; count correct.

f.steps: 1. Browse the certificate wallet home
f.expected: Presentation follows the Apple-Wallet-like card metaphor described in the spec (general check; per-screen Figma validation deferred).
f.notes2: Card border colour + 'Active' badge encode validity — exact mapping unconfirmed; record what you see.

f.steps: 1. Open a certificate card
f.expected: Full field set; Edit (pencil) + Delete (trash) icons; attached Files with View; a QR code.

f.steps: 1. Open an uploaded multi-page certificate
f.expected: Viewer shows all pages, supports zoom/scroll, renders text legibly.

f.steps: 1. On the detail screen, use the QR code / verification link
f.expected: In-app browser opens the verification URL showing 'Document status: VALID', with back/forward/reload. (Backend-dependent.)
f.notes2: Backend-dependent — if the verification server can't be reached, mark Unverifiable.

f.steps: 1. Use Search Certificates with a full term, then a partial term
f.expected: Matching certificates surface; non-matches excluded; clear empty-result state.

f.steps:
1. Open a certificate
2. Use export/share
f.expected: Document exports (share sheet / file) intact and readable outside the app. (Formats unspecified — see thinness report.)

f.steps:
1. Open a certificate
2. Print via the OS print flow
f.expected: Print preview correct; full document prints.

f.steps:
1. Delete a certificate
2. Check the folder and total counts
f.expected: Certificate removed from folder; no orphan entries; viewer no longer opens it.

f.steps:
1. Precondition: a multi-page document uploaded to a chosen folder
2. Reopen the document from its folder
f.expected: Document is in the folder it was tagged to, all pages present, correct orientation, no corruption — exactly what was uploaded.

f.steps:
1. Precondition: several documents uploaded across different folders
2. Enable airplane mode (zero connectivity)
3. Force-quit the app, then cold-start it
4. Open each folder and view every uploaded document
f.expected: Every document opens and renders completely with zero connectivity, including after force-quit + cold start. STOP-SHIP if broken.
f.notes2: STOP-SHIP if any document fails to open fully offline (incl. after force-quit + cold start).

f.steps:
1. Open Service Records
2. Add an entry (vessel, dates, role)
f.expected: Record saves and appears in the listing.

f.steps: 1. Add several records with different dates
f.expected: Listing shows all records, ordered sensibly (chronological).

f.steps:
1. Add a record spanning a known date range
2. Check the sea-days total
f.expected: Sea days match the date range exactly (inclusive rules consistent).

f.steps: 1. Edit an existing record's dates
f.expected: Changes save; listing and sea-days totals update correctly.

f.steps: 1. Delete a record
f.expected: Record removed; totals update; no orphan data.

f.steps:
1. Open the career dashboard with several records
2. Apply filters
f.expected: Chronological listing correct; filters narrow correctly.

f.steps:
1. Set a rank goal
2. Check the goal progress bar
f.expected: Goal saved; progress bar reflects progress logic.

f.steps: 1. Export a report from the dashboard
f.expected: Report exports with correct data. (Format unspecified.)

f.steps: 1. Scan a certificate carrying an issue date, expiry date and certificate number
f.expected: Issue date, expiration date and certificate number are extracted correctly into the entry's fields.
f.notes2: If auto-extraction isn't in this build, mark Skipped/Blocked, not Fail.

f.steps:
1. Enable notifications when prompted
2. Trigger a notification event (per dev guidance)
f.expected: Notification arrives and deep-links sensibly.
f.notes2: Trigger = certificate-expiry push; if none can be triggered on demand, mark Unverifiable.

f.steps:
1. Open profile
2. Change rank and vessel type
3. Save
f.expected: Edits persist and reflect across the app.

f.steps: 1. Open Contact us from settings
f.expected: Page loads and the contact action works (form/email).

f.steps: 1. On an account with NO active subscription, open Terms, Privacy, Contact Us
f.expected: All accessible without hitting the paywall.

f.steps:
1. Sign in and land on My Certificates
2. Go to Profile
3. Tap Log out
f.expected: User is returned cleanly to the sign-in/welcome screen — no black screen, no dead end; signing back in afterwards works.

f.steps:
1. Settings → delete account
2. Confirm
3. Attempt to sign in again
f.expected: Account deletes with clear confirmation; sign-in afterwards fails appropriately.
f.notes2: Destructive — run LAST with a throwaway account.

f.steps:
1. Enable airplane mode
2. Cold-start the app
3. Navigate: dashboard → folders → viewer → profile
f.expected: All primary navigation works offline; no crash, no infinite spinner, no dead screens.

f.steps:
1. Precondition: Device A + Device B on the same account, both online and fully synced
2. Device A: airplane mode
3. Device A: add a new Sea Service Record
4. Verify it appears locally on A
5. Device A back online
f.expected: Record appears on Device B within a few seconds.
f.notes2: Two-device scenario — needs the second device; on a single device mark Unverifiable and narrate.

f.steps:
1. Precondition: Device A + Device B on the same account, both online and fully synced
2. Repeat scenarios 1–3 with Certificates (add, edit, delete) on Device A offline
3. Bring A back online after each
f.expected: Each certificate change syncs to Device B correctly.
f.notes2: Two-device scenario — needs the second device; on a single device mark Unverifiable and narrate.

f.steps:
1. Precondition: server minimum build is 54; skip build 52 entirely (bad publish)
2. Install build 53 from TestFlight (delete the app first, then TestFlight → Wave → Previous Builds → 53)
3. Open the app
f.expected: The forced-version-update screen appears and blocks normal use of the app. (The store Update link won't resolve until the app is live in the App Store — the wall appearing is the check.)
f.notes2: Needs build 53 (skip 52 — bad publish): delete the app, TestFlight → Previous Builds → 53. The store Update link won't work until the app is live — the wall appearing IS the pass.

f.steps:
1. From build 53 showing the update wall, update to build 54 in TestFlight
2. Open the app
3. Background the app, then resume it once
f.expected: Forced-update screen is gone on build 54; the app opens normally. The version check runs on startup and on resume without re-walling a compliant build.
f.notes2: Update to build 54 via TestFlight. Check runs on startup AND on resume from background.

f.steps:
1. On build 53, sign in while online
2. Force-quit the app
3. Enable airplane mode AND turn off Wi-Fi
4. Reopen the app
f.expected: With no connectivity the version check cannot run and does NOT block the user — the app opens with their content available in offline mode (a user with no internet who already got the upgrade request isn't stuck).
f.notes2: Run on build 53 while it is below the minimum.

f.steps:
1. Open the web admin
2. Log in with admin credentials
3. Try a non-admin account
f.expected: Admin signs in; non-admin is rejected.
f.notes2: Web surface — run in a browser; skip this flow if the admin panel isn't part of this release.

f.steps: 1. In admin, open the users table
f.expected: Registered users listed with sensible columns.

f.steps: 1. Open a user from the table
f.expected: Profile viewer shows that user's details.

flows.title: User Flow 1 — Sign-up, onboarding & sign-in
flows.desc: Done when a new user can sign up (email + Google + Apple), verify their email, complete rank and vessel onboarding into a profile, and an existing user can sign in and recover a forgotten password.

items.title: Auth: Welcome screen offers sign up and login
Auth: Vessel type — main categories and subcategories
Auth: Onboarding completes into basic profile

flows.title: User Flow 2 — Certificates wallet
flows.desc: Done when a seafarer can add certificates by upload and scan, organise them across the five categories, view / search / export / print / delete them — and every stored document survives intact and opens fully offline (stop-ship).

items.title: Certificates: Empty state — No Certificates Yet
Certificates: Upload a certificate via file upload
Certificates: Scan a certificate with the camera
Certificates: Multiple certificates in one category
Certificates: QR verification opens in-app browser
Core: View every uploaded document fully offline

flows.title: User Flow 3 — Service records & sea days
flows.desc: Done when service records can be added, listed, edited and deleted, and the sea-days calculation stays correct through each change.

items.title: Service Records: Add a service record
Service Records: Edit a service record recalculates
Service Records: Delete a service record

flows.title: User Flow 4 — Career dashboard, goals & notifications
flows.desc: Done when the career dashboard lists records chronologically with working filters, a rank goal shows progress, career/service reports export, scan auto-extraction fills the form, and a push notification arrives.

items.title: Advanced: Career dashboard — chronological records + filters
Advanced: Set rank goal + progress bar
Advanced: Auto data extraction from scan

flows.title: User Flow 5 — Profile, settings & account
flows.desc: Done when the profile can be viewed and edited, contact and legal pages open, logging out lands cleanly on sign-in, and the delete-account flow removes the account.

items.title: Profile: Legal pages accessible without subscription
Auth: Log out returns to sign-in

flows.title: User Flow 6 — Offline & sync
flows.desc: Done when the app stays usable offline with no lockups, and offline-made changes sync up correctly once connectivity returns.

items.title: Core: App navigates offline without lockups
Offline Sync: Offline certificate CRUD → sync

flows.title: User Flow 7 — Forced version update
flows.desc: Done when a below-minimum build is walled by the forced-update screen, updating clears it, and an already-signed-in offline user is never blocked by the check.

items.title: Forced Update: Outdated build shows the forced-update screen
Forced Update: Updating past the minimum clears the forced-update screen
Forced Update: Offline user is not blocked by the version check

flows.title: User Flow 8 — Admin panel sanity (web)
flows.desc: Done when an admin can sign in on the web panel, see the users table, and open a user's profile.

__m.purpose: Wave — production release acceptance across all main flows (subscriptions excluded; run separately). Happy-path weighted; edges only where release-critical. Narrate the item number before each test.
